title: Copy to container is not consistent  with `docker` for images with no explicit user
body:
### Issue Description

When using `docker cp` into a container based on a image with no explicit user the `uid/gid`s form the tar stream are **always** used irrespective of the the `--archive` CLI flag (the `copyUIDGID` query param).

This **kinda** makes sense, if the user is not set  (even if `root` is implied is such a situation) you cannot chown to the primary User of container since it is not there.

Podman behaves differently if there is no explicit user it fully assumes the `root` user. So when using `podman cp` or calling the compat API the copied files may end up being owned by `root` depending or the `--archive` flag or the `copyUIDGID`  query param.

comments.body:
Hello colleagues,
Can I work on this or someone else is already working for this issue?

I've just found that any file which is copied from host to container with explicit container user has container's user UID and GID has 0 (root).
This behavior is for `podman cp` **regardless** of `--archive` parameter.

Docker behavior preserves source file's UID and GID with `--archive` parameter and set UID to container's user UID and GID has 0 (root).

The issue caused of  `--archive` parameter which is always `true`.

However, there is another issue on the stage of preparing a  tar archive with source files (which will be copied from a host to a container ):

this statement `info, err := os.Lstat(item)` always returns `FileInfo` structure with UID and GID fields set to 0 (root). However, the inode and the device metadata has correct values as it is on my local FS.
On the debug stage it is possible to change the structure's UID and GID fields, then file is copied to a container with the right metadata (UID, GID).

https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/d3b26190acc160bed7f770dc41eb960aa31e9ee8/copier/copier.go#L1329C19-L1341

It seems the issue is due to security constraints.

```
$ getenforce 
Permissive 
```

```
$ podman info
version:
  APIVersion: 6.0.0-dev
  Built: 1764111450
  BuiltTime: Wed Nov 26 00:57:30 2025
  GitCommit: ce0365be7c02316215aa77fedf35ce9b7e2c9a8e-dirty
  GoVersion: go1.25.1
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 6.0.0-dev

```

Could you compare the behavior of rootful vs rootless Podman? While Docker operates in rootful mode by default, Podman defaults to rootless mode (unless executed as root).

This appears to be a fundamental characteristic of how rootless Podman and user namespaces function, rather than a bug in `os.Lstat`.

> Could you compare the behavior of rootful vs rootless Podman? While Docker operates in rootful mode by default, Podman defaults to rootless mode (unless executed as root).
> 
> This appears to be a fundamental characteristic of how rootless Podman and user namespaces function, rather than a bug in `os.Lstat`.

Yes, I will compare behavior of rootful against rootless.
However, I can't get additional file's metadata (UID/GID one) which I'm owner of. Is that OK?

> Could you compare the behavior of rootful vs rootless Podman? While Docker operates in rootful mode by default, Podman defaults to rootless mode (unless executed as root).
> 
> This appears to be a fundamental characteristic of how rootless Podman and user namespaces function, rather than a bug in `os.Lstat`.

Hello @Honny1 
Non-user container with rootful `podman` put UID/GID to 0 (root) regardless of `--archive` argument because, which is default `true`.
I will propose this simple change to fix this issue. With this fix rootful `podman` set the correct UID/GID values of file owner's.

However, `docker` always set UID/GID values of file owner's for user-less container regardless of `--archive` argument.
